items.title: Act Wisely: Cultivating Meta-Cognitive Tool Use in Agentic Multimodal Models
items.authors: Shilin Yan, Jintao Tong, Hongwei Xue et al.
items.abstract: The advent of agentic multimodal models has empowered systems to actively interact with external environments. However, current agents suffer from a profound meta-cognitive deficit: they struggle to arbitrate between leveraging internal knowledge and querying external utilities. Consequently, they f...

items.title: SIM1: Physics-Aligned Simulator as Zero-Shot Data Scaler in Deformable Worlds
items.authors: Yunsong Zhou, Hangxu Liu, Xuekun Jiang et al.
items.abstract: Robotic manipulation with deformable objects represents a data-intensive regime in embodied learning, where shape, contact, and topology co-evolve in ways that far exceed the variability of rigids. Although simulation promises relief from the cost of real-world data acquisition, prevailing sim-to-re...

items.title: Seeing but Not Thinking: Routing Distraction in Multimodal Mixture-of-Experts
items.authors: Haolei Xu, Haiwen Hong, Hongxing Li et al.
items.abstract: Multimodal Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models have achieved remarkable performance on vision-language tasks. However, we identify a puzzling phenomenon termed Seeing but Not Thinking: models accurately perceive image content yet fail in subsequent reasoning, while correctly solving identical problems p...

items.title: AVGen-Bench: A Task-Driven Benchmark for Multi-Granular Evaluation of Text-to-Audio-Video Generation
items.authors: Ziwei Zhou, Zeyuan Lai, Rui Wang et al.
items.abstract: Text-to-Audio-Video (T2AV) generation is rapidly becoming a core interface for media creation, yet its evaluation remains fragmented. Existing benchmarks largely assess audio and video in isolation or rely on coarse embedding similarity, failing to capture the fine-grained joint correctness required...

items.title: GaussiAnimate: Reconstruct and Rig Animatable Categories with Level of Dynamics
items.authors: Jiaxin Wang, Dongxin Lyu, Zeyu Cai et al.
items.abstract: Free-form bones, that conform closely to the surface, can effectively capture non-rigid deformations, but lack a kinematic structure necessary for intuitive control. Thus, we propose a Scaffold-Skin Rigging System, termed "Skelebones", with three key steps: (1) Bones: compress temporally-consistent ...

items.title: ETCH-X: Robustify Expressive Body Fitting to Clothed Humans with Composable Datasets
items.authors: Xiaoben Li, Jingyi Wu, Zeyu Cai et al.
items.abstract: Human body fitting, which aligns parametric body models such as SMPL to raw 3D point clouds of clothed humans, serves as a crucial first step for downstream tasks like animation and texturing. An effective fitting method should be both locally expressive-capturing fine details such as hands and faci...

items.title: Meta-learning In-Context Enables Training-Free Cross Subject Brain Decoding
items.authors: Mu Nan, Muquan Yu, Weijian Mai et al.
items.abstract: Visual decoding from brain signals is a key challenge at the intersection of computer vision and neuroscience, requiring methods that bridge neural representations and computational models of vision. A field-wide goal is to achieve generalizable, cross-subject models. A major obstacle towards this g...

items.title: Demystifying OPD: Length Inflation and Stabilization Strategies for Large Language Models
items.authors: Feng Luo, Yu-Neng Chuang, Guanchu Wang et al.
items.abstract: On-policy distillation (OPD) trains student models under their own induced distribution while leveraging supervision from stronger teachers. We identify a failure mode of OPD: as training progresses, on-policy rollouts can undergo abrupt length inflation, causing truncated trajectories to dominate t...

items.title: Fail2Drive: Benchmarking Closed-Loop Driving Generalization
items.authors: Simon Gerstenecker, Andreas Geiger, Katrin Renz
items.abstract: Generalization under distribution shift remains a central bottleneck for closed-loop autonomous driving. Although simulators like CARLA enable safe and scalable testing, existing benchmarks rarely measure true generalization: they typically reuse training scenarios at test time. Success can therefor...
